```json
  "decision": "fail",

  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Schrader cores left in place during system evacuation",
    "Evacuation cannot achieve deep vacuum with cores installed due to trapped volume and restricted flow",
    "Residual moisture, air, and non-condensables remain in refrigerant circuit",
    "Likely causes reduced capacity, elevated head pressure, acid formation, and compressor damage"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Recover refrigerant in accordance with EPA 608",
    "Remove Schrader cores and pull deep vacuum to manufacturer-specified microns",
    "Use vacuum-rated core removal tools and isolate vacuum gauge from pump",
    "Replace filter-drier, pressurize, leak test, evacuate, and recharge per manufacturer specs"
  ],
  "rationale": "Leaving Schrader cores in during evacuation is a known installation defect. The core and stem create a restricted path and trap air/moisture in the valve body, preventing a deep, uniform vacuum across the entire refrigerant circuit. This violates standard HVAC-R evacuation practice and manufacturer requirements for moisture/non-condensable removal. In service, the result is a contaminated system with elevated risk of acid, compressor degradation, and poor performance.",
  "references": [
    "ACCA",
    "EPA 608",
    "manufacturer specs"
  ]
}
```
